option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Enable logging of deserialization errors during {{C|FinalizeRead}} when loading bones.
option.helptext:
When enabled, metrics won't be turned off during the {{C|FinalizeRead}} portion of bones deserialization.
{{r|Expect the log to quickly fill up.}} Loading bones files with disparate mod configurations results in a lot of non-fatal deserialization errors. They're accounted for in other ways and are an unfortunate trade off to maximise the amount of bones files made available to a given run.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Enable logging of otherwise silenced processes.
option.helptext:
When enabled, metrics won't be turned off during during times they otherwise would.
{{r|Expect the log to quickly fill up.}} The main location this will get verbose is when attempting to discern whether a blueprint or tile are missing for a loaded bones file.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Enable logging the bad word filter results to the log.
option.helptext:
When enabled, the bad word filter will report to the log each of the matches it found in a given string, and the string itself.
This is at the expense of performance, since it would otherwise cease the moment it found its first match.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Enable the bad word filter for non-online bones files.
option.helptext:
When enabled, the bad word filter will be applied to all bones files, including those that are saved to the local device.
This option is primarily for testing the bad word filter without the need to flood a server with filter-triggering bones files.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Enable the bad word filter test words.
{{Y|Bones}}: Pause hoarding bones (saving new bones files).
option.helptext:
When enabled, new Bones files will not be created on player death.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Pause exhuming {{rainbow|Lunar Regent}}s (loading bones files).
option.helptext:
When enabled, new runs will not be assigned a bones file during world-gen.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Pick which {{rainbow|Lunar Regent}} to exhume (pick bones file).
option.helptext:
When enabled, and if entering a new zone, if the zone rolled to load bones, you'll be asked during zone activation which bones file to exhume (load).
You'll only be able to pick from eligible and available bones.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Be asked every zone to pick which {{rainbow|Lunar Regent}} to exhume (force pick bones file).
option.helptext:
When enabled, you'll be asked for every new zone which bones file to exhume (load), irrespective of the chance.
You'll only be able to pick from eligible and available bones.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|Bones}}: Override the debug option "Get prompted to confirm deaths" when "forcing" the "make bones" wish.
option.helptext:
When enabled, using the "make bones" wish with the "die" or "-d" flag set, if the "force" or "-f" flag is set, you won't be asked if you really want to die and dying will move forward as though "Get prompted to confirm deaths" is not enabled.
The default is {{W|Enabled}}.

option.DisplayText: Ask about opting in to the {{black|Osseous Ash}} the next time the game is launched
option.helptext:
When enabled, you'll get a popup when the game first launches asking if you'd like to participate in the {{black|Osseous Ash}} community project. Irrespective of opting in or not, this option will be unchecked so that you won't be asked again.
The {{black|Osseous Ash}} is a shared community bones cloud from which bones can be loaded and encountered. This is a good option for players who want to experience the fun of encountering bones that aren't their own without the requirement that they know others who are active players of Caves of Qud (and users of this mod).
The default is {{W|Enabled}}, automatically switches to {{w|Disabled}} after the popup.

option.DisplayText: Opt in to downloading and encountering bones from the {{black|Osseous Ash}}
option.helptext:
When enabled, and if you are connected to the internet, whenever a list of bones files would normally be collated, the {{black|Osseous Ash}} will also be queried for a selection of bones files to include.
The {{black|Osseous Ash}} is a shared community bones cloud from which bones can be loaded and encountered. This is a good option for players who want to experience the fun of encountering bones that aren't their own without the requirement that they know others who are active players of Caves of Qud (and users of this mod).
When disabled, bones files will only be saved to the local machine (into the "Sync" folder, which syncs with {{b-b-b-y-b sequence|steam}}).
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: Opt in to uploading bones to the {{black|Osseous Ash}}
option.helptext:
When enabled, and if you are connected to the internet, whenever a bones file would normally be saved, that bones file will also be uploaded to the {{black|Osseous Ash}}.
The {{black|Osseous Ash}} is a shared community bones cloud from which bones can be loaded and encountered. This is a good option for players who want to experience the fun of encountering bones that aren't their own without the requirement that they know others who are active players of Caves of Qud (and users of this mod).
When disabled, bones files will only be saved to the local machine (into the "Sync" folder, which syncs with {{b-y-b-b-b sequence|steam}}).
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|[}}Manage {{black|Osseous Ash}} handle{{Y|]}}
option.helptext:
This is the name that will be associated with your bones files when uploaded to the the {{black|Osseous Ash}}.
The default is "{{K|A Mysterious Stranger}}".

option.DisplayText: {{Y|[}}Manage {{black|Osseous Ash}} ID{{Y|]}}
option.helptext:
Use this menu to manage your {{black|Osseous Ash}} ID. You can request a new one (this will disassociate you with the bones you've uplaoded), or copy your current one to your clipboard.
Please note: even without Opting-In to the {{black|Osseous Ash}} community project, several of the mod's components rely on an {{black|Osseous Ash}} ID existing, so one is created for you any time one is absent. Without Opting-In, it will only be used for local operations.

option.DisplayText: {{Y|[}}Manage {{black|Osseous Ash}} hosts{{Y|]}}
option.helptext:
Use this menu to manage your {{black|Osseous Ash}} hosts. You can add new ones, modify them (including disabling them), migrate where the host details are stored, and delete hosts.
Hosts are only ever sent requests if you're opted-in to the {{black|Osseous Ash}} community project, and only for the direction(s) you've opted-in to.
Please note: in the absence of any hosts, this mod will recreate the default one below. If you would like to effectively remove it, you can use this button to modify the default host to be disabled.
The default is {{W|https://osseousash.cloud/}}, saved to [{{G|Synced}}] <...>/CavesOfQud/Synced/OsseousAsh/Hosts.json.

option.DisplayText: Choose chance per zone that bones can be encountered.
{{Y|[}}Manage {{W|custom permyriad}} chance{{Y|]}}
option.helptext:
Enter a custom permyriad chance for a given zone to have bones. Permyriad is like percent, but for 10,000 instead of 100. This allows for percentages with 2 significant digits.
If you want {{W|2.0%}}, enter {{C|200}}. If you want {{W|50.0%}}, enter {{C|5000}}. If you want {{W|0.01%}}, enter {{C|1}}.
Note: if you would like to disable encountering bones altogether, there is a debug option (in the {{c|Debug}} category) to pause bones encounters.
The default is {{W|2.0%}} ({{C|200}}).

option.DisplayText: Choose profanity filter minimum severity level for {{Y|online}} bones files.
option.helptext:
Sets the minimum severity of the profanity filter used when deserializing bones files from online locations. Text components which have "bad words" will be replaced with whatever their blueprint contains if available, otherwise will be replaced with a randomly generated equivalent (such as for titles), or, finally, simply removed.
Currently, the short description, display name, name adjectives, and name titles (honorifics, epithets, etc.) are all checked for moderatable content.
{{c|}} everything - all deserialized objects will be treated as though they contain "bad words". {{c|}} mild - even the least offensive profanity will be filtered. {{c|}} medium - most profanity will be filtered. {{c|}} strong - default, as recommended by the compiler of the list. {{c|}} severe - only the most offensive words will be filtered. {{c|}} no filter - objects will be untouched by the profanity filter.
If you find an online bones file that contains content that you believe the filter has missed, please use the ingame "report bones" inventory action (same place you'd [{{W|l}}]ook at something) to report the bones file to the server admin of any server it's avaialble on, and don't hesitate to reach out to the mod author on the {{b-b-b-y-b sequence|steam}} workshop.
{{W|Please note:}} {{Y|while a significant effort has been made to reduce the likelihood of exposure to potentially offensive content from other users, user discretion is advised when interacting with the online components of this mod. The server software is available on GitHub (https://github.com/UnderDoug/OsseousAsh), and has whitelisting capabilities built in.}}
The default is {{W|strong}}.

option.DisplayText: Choose rough direction you'd like Bones Mode characters' level to be slanted.
option.Values: 4|extremely low,3|very low,2|low,1|slightly low,0|even,-1|slightly high,-2|high,-3|very high,-4|extremely high

option.DisplayText: Enable flashing light effects.
option.helptext:
When enabled, items such as the {{rainbow|Lunar Regent}} masks and objects such as mad {{rainbow|Lunar Regent}}s will have visual effects that might trigger photosensitivity.
When disabled, these effects will be absent.
The default is {{W|Enabled}}.

option.DisplayText: Enable bones from earlier version of the mod to load (provided it's possible).
option.helptext:
When enabled, bones files from previous versions of this mod will be eligible for loading (provided it's possible), albeit at a reduced weight.
When disabled, only bones whose mod version matches the currently enabled bones mod will be eligible for loading, maximising stability.
The default is {{w|Disabled}}.
